messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.subject_clean: Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.body_plain:
This might be a well known issue, but I couldn't spot it in the FAQ or 
bug list or mailing list archives.

If you execute Sys.sleep(60) in R.app, the command can't be interrupted 
by ESC or Cmd-C until it is done.  R.app becomes completely unresponsive.

The number 60 isn't important, as far as I can tell this is true for any 
sleep time.

A simple workaround is to limit the duration of Sys.sleep() calls, e.g. 
using a function like this:

  Sleep <- function(duration) {
   seconds <- floor(duration)
   for (i in seq_len(seconds))
     Sys.sleep(1)
   Sys.sleep(duration - seconds)
  }

R.app handles interrupts every time Sys.sleep returns, so this means the 
biggest delay is 1 second.

In case this isn't universal, here's my session info:

```
R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11)
Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20
Running under: macOS Sequoia 15.7.4

Matrix products: default
BLAS: 
/Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib 

LAPACK: 
/Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib; 
  LAPACK version 3.12.1

locale:
[1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8

time zone: America/Toronto
tzcode source: internal

attached base packages:
[1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base

loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
[1] compiler_4.5.3
```

Duncan Murdoch

by ESC or Cmd-C unt

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.subject_clean: Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.body_plain:
As I've realized, my simple workaround is a little too simple, not 
necessarily matching the requested duration.  A better one looks at the 
elapsed time after the "one second" sleep.

Duncan Murdoch

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.subject_clean: Sys.sleep() in R.app can't be interrupted
messages.body_plain:
Duncan,

thanks. I can replicate it. Something must have happened with the meshing of the event loops, because Sys.sleep() is not supposed to actually sleep the whole time, it should run the system event loop to check for interrupts periodically, but apparently doesn't.

Cheers,
Simon


> On 2 Apr 2026, at 08:32, Duncan Murdoch <murdoch.duncan at gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> This might be a well known issue, but I couldn't spot it in the FAQ or bug list or mailing list archives.
> 
> If you execute Sys.sleep(60) in R.app, the command can't be interrupted by ESC or Cmd-C until it is done.  R.app becomes completely unresponsive.
> 
> The number 60 isn't important, as far as I can tell this is true for any sleep time.
> 
> A simple workaround is to limit the duration of Sys.sleep() calls, e.g. using a function like this:
> 
> Sleep <- function(duration) {
>  seconds <- floor(duration)
>  for (i in seq_len(seconds))
>    Sys.sleep(1)
>  Sys.sleep(duration - seconds)
> }
> 
> R.app handles interrupts every time Sys.sleep returns, so this means the biggest delay is 1 second.
> 
> In case this isn't universal, here's my session info:
> 
> ```
> R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11)
> Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20
> Running under: macOS Sequoia 15.7.4
> 
> Matrix products: default
> BLAS: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib 
> LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib;  LAPACK version 3.12.1
> 
> locale:
> [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8

> time zone: America/Toronto

> 
> attached base packages:

> 
> loaded via a namespace (and not attached):

> ```

> Duncan Murdoch

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Failing to build R-devel: lzma.h missing
messages.subject_clean: Failing to build R-devel: lzma.h missing
messages.body_plain:
I'm trying to build R-devel.  I used this command line to configure it:

../R-devel-src//configure -C --enable-R-shlib --enable-memory-profiling 
--x-includes=/opt/X11/include --x-libraries=/opt/X11/lib 
--with-tcl-config=/opt/R/arm64/lib/tclConfig.sh 
--with-tk-config=/opt/R/arm64/lib/tkConfig.sh 
PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/opt/R/arm64/lib/pkgconfig:/usr/lib/pkgconfig

(I found that in the old config.log, with "pkg-config" in the 
PKG_CONFIG_PATH instead of "pkgconfig".  Neither one works.  I think it 
also matches the suggestion in the R-admin manual.)  Is there something 
missing?  It fails with this error at the end:


   checking for lzma_version_number in -llzma... yes
   checking for lzma.h... no
   configure: error: "liblzma library and headers are required"

But I see lzma.h in /opt/R/arm64/include, and that's where pkg-config 
--cflags liblzma says it should be.  I tried removing PKG_CONFIG_PATH 
and got the same result.

Duncan Murdoch

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Failing to build R-devel: lzma.h missing
messages.subject_clean: Failing to build R-devel: lzma.h missing
messages.body_plain:
I have

$ cat ~/r-devel/BUILD/config.site 
CPPFLAGS=-I/opt/R/x86_64/include
LDFLAGS=-L/opt/R/x86_64/lib
with_tcl_config=/opt/R/x86_64/lib/tclConfig.sh
with_tk_config=/opt/R/x86_64/lib/tkConfig.sh

(even though Simon says I shouldn't use config files...)

I.e. you may need to set the *FLAGS in your setup (switch out the arch of course).

-pd

Thanks, that was it.  I had something similar to yours, but mine 
depended on $LOCAL as in the R-admin value, and I didn't have $LOCAL set 
properly.

Duncan

Thanks, that was it.  I had something similar to yours, but mine 
depended on $LOCAL as in the R-admin value, and I didn't have $LOCAL set 
properly.

Duncan

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] utils:::.available.both() breaks install.packages() on Mac sonoma-arm64
messages.subject_clean: utils:::.available.both() breaks install.packages() on Mac sonoma-arm64
messages.body_plain:
With?R 4.6 >= r89829? we're seeing this install.packages() error on Mac 
sonoma-arm64 in various places:

 ? ? Error in av.src[pkg.bin, "Version"] : subscript out of bounds

This happens for example with install.packages("OSTA.data", 
repos=BiocManager::repositories()).

Also started to happen in this GitHub action a few days ago: 
https://github.com/HyrienLab/igblastr/actions/runs/24495733061/job/71590105822

The error occurs in the new utils:::.available.both() function 
introduced at r89827 in trunk and?r89829 in R-4-6-branch (file 
src/library/utils/R/packages.R). It can simply be reproduced with:

utils:::.available.both("https://cran.r-project.org")

 ? ? # Error in av.src[pkg.bin, "Version"] : subscript out of bounds

It seems that the function should be able to handle the situation where 
some binary packages in the repo don't have a corresponding source 
package. Something like this maybe:

.available.both <- function(repos, method, ...) {
 ? ? ## "both" gets complicated as we have to use available.packages twice
 ? ? fields <- c(tools:::.get_standard_repository_db_fields(), "File", 
"Built")
 ? ? av.src <- available.packages(contriburl = contrib.url(repos, 
"source"), method = method, fields = fields, ...)
 ? ? av.bin <- available.packages(contriburl = contrib.url(repos, 
"binary"), method = method, fields = fields, ...)
 ? ? ## we keep things simple in that we use source unless there is the 
same binary version
 ? ? ## so that we can get at the Built field. This is more simple than 
in install.packages
 ? ? ## since we only care about determining whether a package is new, 
but not which type is
 ? ? ## preferred - install.packages will sort that out.
 ? ? all_src_pkgs <- row.names(av.src)
 ? ? all_bin_pkgs <- row.names(av.bin)
 ? ? dual_pkgs <- intersect(all_src_pkgs, all_bin_pkgs)
 ? ? src_ver <- av.src[dual_pkgs, "Version"]
 ? ? bin_ver <- av.bin[dual_pkgs, "Version"]
 ? ? use_bin <- as.numeric_version(bin_ver) >= as.numeric_version(src_ver)
 ? ? ## If for some reason 'src_ver' or 'bin_ver' contain NAs, they 
would propagate
 ? ? ## to 'use_bin', but can this ever happen?
 ? ? use_bin[is.na(use_bin)] <- TRUE
 ? ? src_pkgs <- c(setdiff(all_src_pkgs, dual_pkgs), dual_pkgs[!use_bin])
 ? ? bin_pkgs <- c(setdiff(all_bin_pkgs, dual_pkgs), dual_pkgs[use_bin])
 ? ? ## Returned matrix has 1 row per package in 'union(all_src_pkgs, 
all_bin_pkgs)'.
 ? ? rbind(av.src[src_pkgs, ], av.bin[bin_pkgs, ])
}

Best,

H.

-- 
Herv? Pag?s

Bioconductor Core Team
hpages.on.github at gmail.com
messages.body_snippet:

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac]  utils:::.available.both() breaks install.packages() on Mac sonoma-arm64
messages.subject_clean: utils:::.available.both() breaks install.packages() on Mac sonoma-arm64
messages.body_plain:
Also

 ? ? rbind(av.src[src_pkgs, ], av.bin[bin_pkgs, ])

should be

 ? ? rbind(av.src[src_pkgs, , drop=FALSE], av.bin[bin_pkgs, , drop=FALSE])

or bad things will happen if src_pkgs or bin_pkgs have length 1.

H.
